quality_policy.notes: Primary 3B-token production builds should use top-engine self-play or elite human tournament/player subsets. General public archives are not primary training sources unless a strict top-player/top-engine filter is documented in the source entry.

chess.name: Stockfish fishtest LTC PGNs
chess.license: LGPL-3.0 (applied to dataset on HuggingFace, separate from engine GPL-3.0)
chess.recommended_subset: 2022+ tests, passed status, avg depth >= 15, game length >= 20 moves
chess.annotations: Per-move eval (centipawns), search depth, time spent: {-0.91/21 1.749s}
chess.notes: Best verified volume source. Each game has per-move engine evaluation and depth annotations. Filter by test-pass status from JSON metadata, recency (2022+), and minimum search depth. ~20M filtered games recommended for the 3B mix.

chess.name: Leela Chess Zero self-play training PGNs
chess.license: Engine is GPLv3; training data/PGNs have NO explicit license statement. Game outputs are not derivative works of GPL software. Contact Lc0 team for explicit confirmation before redistribution.
chess.notes: Neural MCTS engine with fundamentally different style from Stockfish alpha-beta. Critical for style diversity. Early networks are weak — filter to latest 20-30% of generations. May contain many short draws at high strength.

chess.name: Lichess Elite Database
chess.license: CC0 (inherited from Lichess CC0 public domain data)
chess.notes: GM/IM-level human games. Provides opening diversity and practical play patterns that engines lack. Use ~750K games as 3% of the 3B mix for human-style diversity.

chess.name: TCEC engine championship PGNs
chess.notes: Highest-quality multi-engine tournament games at very long time controls. Too small for primary volume but invaluable as evaluation seed and for inter-engine style diversity (games between DIFFERENT engines).

chess.name: ICCF Correspondence Chess
chess.license: Access requires ICCF registration or Chessify subscription; redistribution terms unclear
chess.notes: Near-engine-quality play (engine-assisted correspondence, days per move). Represents exhaustive positional analysis. Needs license investigation before use.

chess.name: Raw Lichess standard rated games
chess.notes: Do not use unfiltered for primary training. Only use with explicit rating/time-control/cheat-filter gates.

shogi.name: AobaZero self-play CSA archives
shogi.license: Public domain (game records); GPL v3 (engine code only). README states: 'USIエンジンであるaobazはGPL v3です。それ以外はpublic domainです'
shogi.download_method: Google Drive via rclone (Records01 folder). See: https://tokumini.hatenablog.com/entry/2020/09/11/140000
shogi.annotations: MCTS visit counts for candidate moves in CSA comments
shogi.notes: AlphaZero-style self-play, 3,460+ network generations. Late-training games are superhuman. Final network ~137 Elo above elmo. Filter out ILLEGAL_ACTION games. 39.8M games x 258 tok = ~10.3B tokens, 3.4x target.

shogi.name: Floodgate yearly archives
shogi.license: Public archive; preserve provenance and checksums
shogi.volume: ~1.2-1.8 million games total (2008-2026), ~100K-200K after R3000+ filter
shogi.filtering: Both players R3000+, 50+ moves, resignation finish (yields ~18% survival rate per TadaoYamaoka)
shogi.notes: Multi-engine diversity supplement. Top engines include dlshogi, AobaZero, Kristallweizen, Suisho. Use only with documented rating/engine allowlist.

shogi.name: WCSC championship CSA archives
shogi.notes: World Computer Shogi Championship official game records. Post-2023 games include evaluation values and PV lines. Highest quality multi-engine tournament games. Too small for volume but excellent evaluation seed.

shogi.name: dlshogi / GCT hcpe3 training data
shogi.license: No explicit license stated; availability not guaranteed
shogi.notes: EXCLUDED: hcpe3 stores individual (position, move, eval, result) tuples, NOT game-level move sequences. Cannot reconstruct sequential game records for LM training. The original CSA self-play games are not publicly available.

go.name: KataGo distributed self-play training games (SGF)
go.license: MIT-style for networks; data policy permits use 'by anyone' for training and research. DISCLAIMER.txt is standard as-is waiver.
go.volume: 94.6 million total games (~47M at 19x19), 4.69 billion training rows
go.recommended_subset: 19x19 only, b28c512+ and b40c768 networks (~12M games)
go.annotations: MCTS visit counts, value estimates, rules/komi per game
go.notes: Primary Go source. SGF files available directly at katagoarchive.org — no format conversion needed. 942 networks spanning random to superhuman. Filter to strong late-training networks and 19x19 for primary corpus. ~34,500 new games/day ongoing.

go.name: Leela Zero self-play SGF archives
go.notes: Pure AlphaGo Zero approach (single board size, no human knowledge). Project ended 2021-02-15. Different training methodology from KataGo adds style diversity. Final network #287 reached Elo 16,726.

go.name: ELF OpenGo self-play SGFs
go.license: BSD (code); game data publicly released by FAIR
go.notes: Facebook AI Research superhuman Go engine. Different architecture and training from KataGo/LZ. Project archived Dec 2020 — verify download URL accessibility. Use ~500K games for additional engine style diversity.

go.name: Joe's Go Database (JGDB)
go.notes: Best free professional game collection. Provides opening diversity and historical strategic patterns that engine self-play lacks.

go.name: CWI Japanese Professional Go Games
go.notes: Japanese professional game archive. Complements JGDB for professional game coverage.

go.name: Tygem 9D vs 9D dataset
go.license: GPL-3.0 (tools); game data from server scraping — redistribution ambiguous
go.format: gib (Tygem native, converter to SGF included)
go.notes: Strong amateur/semi-pro games from Tygem server. Highest-ranked human online play. Use ~500K for human-style diversity.

go.name: KGS 6d+ game archives
go.notes: Strong amateur games. AlphaGo's supervised learning used 160K KGS 6d-9d games.

go.name: ZhiziGo KataGo self-play mirror
go.notes: EXCLUDED: Mirror of kata1 data from katagotraining.org. ZhiziGo contributed compute/optimizer improvements to kata1 run. Do not double-count with primary KataGo source. Useful only as alternate download mirror.

go.name: OGS public SGF collection
go.notes: Do not use unfiltered. Includes all skill levels from beginners. Only use with strict rank filters.

othello.name: Egaroucid self-play generation
othello.license: GPL-3.0 engine; generated game records are not derivative works and can be freely used/redistributed
othello.volume_estimate: 40M+ games at level 11 (superhuman: 11-ply midgame + 25-ply perfect endgame)

generation_config.parallelization: N single-threaded instances, one per core

othello.notes: Strongest open-source Othello engine (beats Edax at all levels). Level 11 produces superhuman play. Use XOT openings for diversity.

othello.name: Edax self-play generation
othello.license: GPL-3.0 engine; generated game records are not derivative works
othello.volume_estimate: 2M games at level 15 for style diversity
othello.notes: Second-strongest open-source Othello engine. Use at level 15 as style-diversity supplement.

othello.name: Egaroucid released 2M game transcripts
othello.license: Free to use for training; redistribution prohibited
othello.notes: Pre-generated self-play transcripts. Usable for training but cannot be redistributed in raw form.

othello.name: WTHOR tournament database
othello.license: Public download from FFOthello; verify redistribution terms
othello.notes: Highest-quality human tournament records. Invaluable as evaluation seed and human-style diversity.

othello.name: Random/synthetic public Othello datasets
othello.notes: EXCLUDED: Generated by random legal moves (othello_world lineage). Not suitable for policy imitation.

poker.name: ACPC NLHE hands (PHH/Zenodo corpus)
poker.volume: 160,575,000 unique NLHE hands (278.8M total with duplicates)
poker.recommended_subset: ACPC NLHE 2014-2017 (86.7M unique, strongest agent generations with EV estimates)
poker.notes: Annual Computer Poker Competition engine-vs-engine hands. 160M unique NLHE provides 43x the 3.7M needed for 3B tokens.

poker.name: ACPC FLHE hands (PHH/Zenodo corpus)
poker.notes: Fixed-limit hold'em variant from ACPC. Include for game-variant diversity.

poker.name: Pluribus released hands
poker.license: Verify Brown and Sandholm supplementary data terms
poker.notes: Highest-quality 6-player poker data from Pluribus (Science 2019). Invaluable as evaluation seed.

poker.name: Slumbot2019 self-play generation
poker.notes: 2018 ACPC champion, strongest openly available HUNLHE agent. C++ CFR/MCCFR. ACPC corpus volume makes this unnecessary for 3B target.

poker.name: Generic online poker hand histories in PHH corpus
poker.notes: Do not use unfiltered for primary policy training; stakes and player quality are mixed.

bridge.name: Ben (lorserker/ben) self-play generation
bridge.license: GPL-3.0 engine; generated game records (PBN output) are not derivative works

generation_config.estimated_speed: 1-5 deals/sec per process, 8-40 deals/sec with 8 processes

bridge.notes: Only open-source engine providing both NN bidding and DDS-assisted play. Primary volume generation path.

bridge.name: OpenSpiel WBridge5 1M dataset
bridge.license: Verify OpenSpiel dataset license (Apache-2.0 for code)
bridge.notes: Pre-generated WBridge5 self-play data from Lockhart et al. 2020. Download from GCS.

bridge.name: DDS v3.0.0 double-dummy solving
bridge.notes: Double-dummy solver only (no bidding). 8-10 deals/sec. Use with Ben bidding NN for hybrid pipeline.

bridge.name: Pgx precomputed DDS results
bridge.notes: Pre-solved DD trick tables. No bidding/play. Useful as seeds for Ben bidding overlay.

bridge.name: Pavlicek solved deals library
bridge.notes: Pre-solved DD results only. Fastest hybrid generation: overlay Ben bidding NN (160-400 deals/sec).

bridge.name: BBO Vugraph tournament archives
bridge.license: Verify BBO/Vugraph terms before bulk redistribution
bridge.volume: ~600,000 boards (Bermuda Bowl, Venice Cup, NABC, etc.)
bridge.notes: Elite human tournament records with full bidding + play. LIN format requires conversion to PBN.

bridge.name: Vugraph Project / ComputerBridge PBN finals
bridge.license: Verify event and archive redistribution terms
bridge.notes: World Championship, Bermuda Bowl, Venice Cup PBN files. High-quality seed.
